methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
SwarmFarm Robotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised AU$30M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of six same-stage private peers in the robotics sector, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat, alongside one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ).

The peer-median method anchors the analysis. The six comparable Series B robotics companies exhibit a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. This wide dispersion—driven primarily by 1X Technologies' $10.0B valuation at the upper bound—reflects material heterogeneity in company maturity, commercial traction, and market positioning within the frontier robotics space. The peer median of $1.1B provides the primary quantitative reference point given the availability of comparable data.

Based on peer triangulation, SwarmFarm Robotics' valuation range is supported at $914M to $1.2B, with a median of $1.1B. This range reflects the central tendency of the comp set but carries meaningful uncertainty. The wide dispersion of peer valuations, particularly the substantial gap between the $500M–$1.8B cluster and the $10.0B outlier, indicates that stage-matched peers alone may inadequately capture company-specific factors including technology differentiation, customer concentration, and path to revenue profitability. Additional diligence on SwarmFarm's commercial pipeline and technical capability relative to named peers would refine this estimate.
